examples.title: Algorithm: JToggleButton Demo
examples.description: A pseudocode demonstrating the basic functionality of a JToggleButton
examples.code:
ALGORITHM JToggleButtonDemo
INPUT: None
OUTPUT: A window with a toggle button and a color panel

BEGIN
 CREATE a new window with a specified title
 SET the default close operation to exit the application when the window is closed
 SET the window's size and layout
 CREATE a new panel for color display
 SET the panel's preferred size and initial background color
 ADD the panel to the window
 CREATE a new toggle button with specified text and initial state
 ADD an item listener to the toggle button to handle state change events
 ADD the toggle button to the window
 SET the window to be visible
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode outlines the steps to create a simple JToggleButton demo, including setting up a window, adding a color panel and a toggle button, and handling the button's state change events.

examples.title: Java: JToggleButton Demo
examples.description: A Java implementation demonstrating the basic functionality of a JToggleButton
examples.explanation: This Java code creates a window with a toggle button and a color panel. When the toggle button is clicked, the color panel's background color changes, and the button's text is updated accordingly.

examples.title: Java: JToggleButton Group Demo
examples.description: A Java implementation demonstrating the use of ButtonGroup with JToggleButtons
examples.explanation: This Java code creates a window with three JToggleButtons grouped using a ButtonGroup. Only one button can be selected at a time, demonstrating the radio button-like behavior.
